title: Word Definitions, Tokenization, Boundaries, and Offsets · Behavior Case: Apostrophe Hyphen Policy
summary: Whitespace splitting treats punctuation as words, a regex drops apostrophes and non-Latin scripts, hyphen policy changes totals silently, and empty tokens enter the frequency table. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the apostrophe hyphen policy increment in tested word and token contract. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.
Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-apostrophe-hyphen-policy — Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-tokenization-word-policy-5
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-tokenization-word-policy-5.
# Case 9036: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def worked_bookbot_tokenization_word_policy_5(text_length: int = 42, token_count: int = 7, empty_tokens: int = 0, offsets_valid: bool = True, word_policy_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "policy5dekrow",
        "revision9036",
        "commandworked59036",
        "report5dekrow9036",
        "test9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if text_length < 0 or token_count < 0:
        return False, token_count, "token-count"
    if empty_tokens > 0:
        return False, empty_tokens, "empty-token"
    if not offsets_valid:
        return False, token_count, "offset-contract"
    if not word_policy_named:
        return False, token_count, "undefined-word"
    assert token_count <= text_length
    return True, token_count, "bounded-tokens"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend the Apostrophe Hyphen Policy model · Theory Apostrophe Hyphen Policy
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Apostrophe Hyphen Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Bookbot team handles case 9082 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to tokenize under a named word policy with nonempty tokens and valid source offsets; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend the Apostrophe Hyphen Policy model · Theory Apostrophe Hyphen Policy.
steps.why: Retrieving Apostrophe Hyphen Policy through the concrete tested word and token contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend the Apostrophe Hyphen Policy model · Theory Apostrophe Hyphen Policy, keep Apostrophe Hyphen Policy connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Apostrophe Hyphen Policy model · Theory Apostrophe Hyphen Policy, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Apostrophe Hyphen Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Apostrophe Hyphen Policy model · Theory Apostrophe Hyphen Policy, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 9082 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to tokenize under a named word policy with nonempty tokens and valid source offsets; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Apostrophe Hyphen Policy model · Theory Apostrophe Hyphen Policy, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Removing all punctuation cannot change word meaning or count.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-apostrophe-hyphen-policy-1talpj.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-apostrophe-hyphen-policy-1talpj(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_apostrophe_hyphen_policy_1talpj\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?text_length < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?empty_tokens > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not offsets_valid)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not word_policy_named)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
